plugins.name: Plugin Hub
plugins.author: 6ix
plugins.description: Browse, install, and toggle Paralives mods from inside the game. Auto-updates itself and your installed mods on each launch â€” no manual file copying after the first install. Also fixes the Steam-offline boot hang so the game launches without internet. v1.0.21: retired mods are now hidden, and any installed copy is cleaned up automatically.

plugins.name: Paralives Optimisation
plugins.author: 6ix
plugins.description: Boosts FPS and adds a full Video settings overhaul. New this version: a Game Resolution slider that renders the 3D world at a lower resolution and upscales it to your screen while the UI stays razor sharp, plus separate MSAA and a cheaper post-process anti-aliasing option, image sharpening, and on/off toggles for bloom, ambient occlusion, depth of field, vignette, chromatic aberration, film grain, and shadow distance. Ships with anti-aliasing off, bloom off, and borderless window by default for the smoothest out-of-the-box performance. Under the hood it replaces several per-frame hot paths with allocation-free versions and mutes per-frame debug overhead. Every patch is fail-safe: if a game update changes a hooked method, that patch falls back to vanilla behaviour and logs once. Updated for game v0.1.4.

plugins.name: Not Enough Workshop Mods
plugins.author: 6ix
plugins.description: Retired: this fix is now built into FixMyReimport, so you only need that one. The hub hides this entry and removes any installed copy automatically once it updates.Stops the game from deleting your Steam Workshop mods. On a Steam hiccup at launch the game can wipe ALL your locally-installed workshop mods at once â€” this makes that impossible: mods are only ever removed against a complete, confirmed subscription list, never on a failed, empty, or timed-out one, and it retries quietly in the background until Steam responds. A second independent safety net blocks any mass-delete even if a future game update breaks the main fix. Mods that fail to load show a red (Skipped) / amber (Partial) tag in My Mods instead of vanishing silently. Updated for game v0.1.3. Cross-platform (Windows/Mac/Linux/Steam Deck).

plugins.name: FixMyReimport
plugins.author: 6ix
plugins.description: Keeps your game from getting stuck on the loading screen, and protects your Steam Workshop mods from being deleted when Steam hiccups at launch (this now includes the old Not Enough Workshop Mods fix, so you only need this one). If a mod won't load, gets the game stuck on the 'Reimporting Assets' screen, or a save is damaged, your game no longer freezes â€” it either skips the bad part and keeps going, or, if a load truly can't finish, brings you back to the main menu with a Retry button instead of leaving you stuck. Even with a big pile of mods where several are broken, your game still starts and loads all the good ones, and notes which one caused the trouble. Broken mods are tried again next launch, so a one-time hiccup (like a mod still downloading) isn't switched off for good. Updated for game v0.1.3.

plugins.name: Safer Saves
plugins.author: 6ix
plugins.description: Protects your saved games from being lost or corrupted. Every save is written safely off to the side and only swapped in once it's fully on disk, so a crash or power loss mid-save never leaves you with a broken file. It also quietly keeps your last 5 saves as backups, checks each save before loading, and â€” if one ever looks damaged â€” offers to restore the most recent good backup (keeping your damaged files in a '.corrupt' folder just in case). Press F8 any time for an instant manual backup. Works on its own in the background; safe to add or remove at any time.

plugins.name: Stats Overlay
plugins.author: 6ix
plugins.description: Unity-Editor-style stats panel â€” FPS, CPU/render times, draw calls, triangles, audio level, animation counts. Top-left overlay. Press F3 to toggle.

plugins.name: What Is My Loading Doing
plugins.author: 6ix
plugins.description: Shows you what the game is actually doing on the loading screen â€” like 'Loading furniture (142/300)', 'Loading characters', or 'Building terrain' â€” instead of just a spinning icon. If a load ever gets stuck, it tells you exactly which Workshop item caused it, so you know what to remove.

plugins.name: Direct Control
plugins.author: Rockz
plugins.description: Move your selected Para yourself with WASD instead of clicking around. Adds a third-person camera you can swing with the mouse, an in-game settings menu with fully rebindable keys (AZERTY/QWERTZ friendly), sprinting with a real running animation, mood-based walk styles, a toggle to stop furniture/walls fading, auto-opening doors, and smoother collision. F1 toggles it on/off, F4 opens settings. A community collaboration â€” authored and maintained by Rockz.

plugins.name: Traits
plugins.author: xSauroXplayer
plugins.description: Adds a traits panel to character creation so you can give your Parafolk personality traits, and they save with your game. The whole mod is a single file with all 521 icons built in, and it creates an editable Resources folder on first launch so you can add, remove, or re-skin traits yourself. It keeps its trait data in its own file, so it never touches your game saves. Authored and maintained by xSauroXplayer.
